_comment: Hand-curated layer of docs/capabilities.json (capability-config.md §2 p.3). The emitter (stapel_auth/_capabilities.py) merges this over the derived axes; an axis present in conf.py DEFAULTS but missing here (or vice versa) is a loud emission error. Keep business_label/summary in business language — they surface in CTO conversations, not in code.
provides: User authentication and account access: sign-up and sign-in with email or phone one-time codes, passwords, social (OAuth) accounts, corporate SSO (SAML/OIDC), magic links, QR hand-off and passkeys; guest (anonymous) access; two-factor authentication and step-up verification; session and device management with a security audit trail.

requires.reason: comm bus (events/functions), JWT provider, verification mechanism, OAuth provider registry
default REREGISTRATION_MODEL target (lazy dotted path); co-mounted under /auth/api/ in the contract harness
delivers the OTP / magic-link / security emails and SMS this module requests via notification.requested

extension_points.name: OAUTH_PROVIDER_CLASSES
extension_points.summary: Register additional OAuth providers (subclass stapel_core.oauth.OAuthProvider in the host app, append the dotted path) — no fork.

extension_points.name: REREGISTRATION_MODEL
extension_points.summary: Swap the model that stores re-registration hashes; resolved lazily, stapel-gdpr is not a hard dependency.

extension_points.name: AUTH_USER_MODEL
extension_points.summary: Standard Django user swap — subclass stapel-core's AbstractStapelUser; this module only ever uses get_user_model().

extension_points.name: serializer_seams
extension_points.summary: Every user-facing view declares *_request/_response_serializer_class seams (SerializerSeamsMixin) — subclass the view, override the attribute, mount via the URL factories.

extension_points.name: STAPEL_VERIFICATION.EXTRA_FACTORS
extension_points.summary: Add step-up verification factors to the stapel-core registry alongside the built-ins this module registers (otp_email, otp_phone, totp, passkey).

extension_points.name: url_factories
extension_points.summary: Compose your own URLconf from the get_*_urls() factories so disabled features 404 (include('stapel_auth.urls') mounts everything with per-request gating).

AUTH_PHONE_REGISTRATION.summary: New users can create an account by confirming their phone number with a one-time SMS code.

AUTH_EMAIL_REGISTRATION.summary: New users can create an account by confirming their email with a one-time code.

AUTH_OAUTH_REGISTRATION.summary: New users can create an account through Google, GitHub, Apple and other configured OAuth providers.

AUTH_SSO_REGISTRATION.business_label: Automatic accounts for corporate SSO users
AUTH_SSO_REGISTRATION.summary: Employees signing in through their company identity provider (SAML/OIDC) get an account provisioned on first login.

AUTH_PASSWORD_REGISTRATION.summary: New users can register with a login and password. Off by default — passwordless methods are the house default.

AUTH_PHONE_LOGIN.summary: Existing users sign in by receiving a one-time SMS code.

AUTH_EMAIL_LOGIN.summary: Existing users sign in by receiving a one-time code by email.

AUTH_OAUTH_LOGIN.summary: Existing users sign in through the configured OAuth providers.

AUTH_SSO_LOGIN.summary: Users of onboarded organizations sign in through their company identity provider (SAML/OIDC), configured per organization in the database.

AUTH_PASSWORD_LOGIN.summary: Existing users sign in with login and password (plus change/reset flows). Off by default.

AUTH_QR_LOGIN.business_label: Sign-in by QR code from another device
AUTH_QR_LOGIN.summary: A signed-in device shows a QR code; scanning it signs the user in on the new device.

AUTH_PASSKEY_LOGIN.summary: Users sign in with platform passkeys (Face ID, fingerprint, hardware security keys) via WebAuthn; also usable as a second factor.

AUTH_MAGIC_LINK_LOGIN.business_label: Sign-in via a one-time email link
AUTH_MAGIC_LINK_LOGIN.summary: Users sign in by clicking a short-lived link sent to their email — no code entry.

AUTH_ANONYMOUS.summary: Visitors get a temporary anonymous account (e.g. to browse or fill a cart) that can later be upgraded to a full account; guest data merges on upgrade.

AUTH_TOTP.business_label: Two-factor authentication with an authenticator app
AUTH_TOTP.summary: Users can protect their account with time-based one-time codes (Google Authenticator etc.); also the factor behind step-up checks on sensitive actions.

OAUTH_STEP_UP.summary: Require a two-factor code even after a social (OAuth) login. Off by default — the provider already authenticated the user.
OAUTH_STEP_UP.behavior: When on, a successful OAuth login with a TOTP-enrolled account returns a TOTP challenge instead of tokens.

PASSWORD_LOGIN_STEP_UP.summary: Require a two-factor code after password login for TOTP-enrolled accounts. On by default — a password alone is phishable.
PASSWORD_LOGIN_STEP_UP.behavior: When on, a successful password login with a TOTP-enrolled account returns a TOTP challenge instead of tokens.

AUTH_EMAIL_PLACEMENT.business_label: Where the email sign-in method appears
AUTH_EMAIL_PLACEMENT.summary: Controls whether the email one-time-code method renders inline in the primary sign-in tabs, behind the "more" menu, or in the bottom button row. Purely presentational.

AUTH_PHONE_PLACEMENT.business_label: Where the phone sign-in method appears
AUTH_PHONE_PLACEMENT.summary: Controls whether the phone one-time-code method renders inline in the primary sign-in tabs, behind the "more" menu, or in the bottom button row. Purely presentational.

AUTH_PASSWORD_PLACEMENT.business_label: Where the password sign-in method appears
AUTH_PASSWORD_PLACEMENT.summary: Controls whether password sign-in renders inline in the primary sign-in tabs, behind the "more" menu, or in the bottom button row. Purely presentational.

AUTH_MAGIC_LINK_PLACEMENT.business_label: Where the email-link sign-in method appears
AUTH_MAGIC_LINK_PLACEMENT.summary: Controls whether the one-time email-link method renders inline in the primary sign-in tabs, behind the "more" menu, or in the bottom button row. Purely presentational.

AUTH_SSO_PLACEMENT.summary: Controls whether corporate SSO renders inline in the primary sign-in tabs, behind the "more" menu, or in the bottom button row. Purely presentational.

AUTH_OAUTH_PLACEMENT.summary: Controls whether social (OAuth) sign-in renders inline in the primary sign-in tabs, behind the "more" menu, or in the bottom button row. Purely presentational.

AUTH_QR_PLACEMENT.summary: Controls whether QR hand-off sign-in renders inline in the primary sign-in tabs, behind the "more" menu, or in the bottom button row. Purely presentational.

AUTH_PASSKEY_PLACEMENT.summary: Controls whether passkey sign-in renders inline in the primary sign-in tabs, behind the "more" menu, or in the bottom button row. Purely presentational.
